title: Internal boundary manifest and open/local capability matrix
description: Boundary schema for the internal-boundary capability matrix. One row per protected surface (shell, editor, workspace, search, terminal, support, etc.). Each row carries one boundary_class (local_only, provider_linked, managed, mirrored, unsupported), the deployment profiles it claims to operate on, the residual-dependency classes it relies on with a per-class posture drawn from the residual_dependency_ledger, the public-truth fields the row carries on About / Help / service-health / support / release surfaces, and a named failure drill. Closed vocabularies frozen here are the cross-tool boundary that runtime surfaces, docs, release artifacts, support exports, and CI validation all consume. Adding a new boundary_class, residual_dependency_class, posture_class, deployment_profile, surface_family, truth_field, or failure_drill_id is additive-minor and bumps schema_version; repurposing an existing value is breaking and opens a decision row.

status.description: Lifecycle state of this matrix instance. The M1 draft seeds the contract; ratification of individual rows happens via decision rows on the matrix, not by deleting them.

boundary_class_vocabulary.description: Closed boundary-class vocabulary every row resolves against. local_only: capability MUST work with no network, no sign-in, no hosted service. provider_linked: optional user-supplied provider (BYOK / customer IdP / local model) attaches to the local surface; without a provider the surface narrows but is not removed. managed: hosted-only convenience layered on a self-hostable protocol; the underlying workflow remains reachable via the self-hostable path. mirrored: reachable via a signed offline mirror or bundle in constrained-connectivity profiles. unsupported: explicitly out of scope for the named milestone; reserved row prevents implicit drift.

deployment_profile_vocabulary.description: Deployment-profile ids the matrix is allowed to cite. MUST match artifacts/governance/deployment_profiles.yaml#deployment_profile_vocabulary verbatim.

residual_dependency_class_vocabulary.description: Residual-dependency classes a surface row may declare. MUST be a subset of artifacts/governance/residual_dependencies.yaml#dependency_class_vocabulary so rows do not invent new dependency classes here.

posture_class_vocabulary.description: Per-residual-dependency posture classes. MUST match artifacts/governance/residual_dependencies.yaml#posture_class_vocabulary verbatim.

surface_family_vocabulary.description: Surface families the matrix enumerates. M1 seeds one row per family; later milestones may introduce additive members.

truth_field_vocabulary.description: Public-truth fields a row may claim to populate on Help/About/service-health/support-export/release surfaces. Closed vocabulary so consumers do not invent parallel field names.

manifest_status_vocabulary.description: Per-row manifest status. The M1 draft seeds rows as proposed; ratification flips to accepted; rows do not delete (use superseded).

required_surface_family_coverage.description: Surface families the M1 matrix MUST seed at least one row for; gaps fail the validation lane.

rows.description: Capability-matrix rows. One row per protected M1 surface.

local_core_continuity.description: Statement of what still works locally when every optional service this row touches is absent. For local_only rows this restates the invariant. For all other boundary_class values it MUST name the local-safe baseline so absence narrows a claim rather than silently failing.

residual_dependencies.description: Residual-dependency declarations consumed against the residual-dependency ledger. May be empty for local_only rows with no residual surface.

carries_truth_fields.description: Closed-vocab fields the surface row promises to surface honestly when consumed by Help/About/service-health/support/release tooling. Every row MUST declare at least one truth field so 'inspectable boundary truth' is non-trivial.

absence_narrows_to.description: Narrowed claim the capability carries when its named optional dependencies are absent; pairs with residual_dependencies entries.

per_profile_posture.description: Per-profile posture for this residual dependency. Every profile entry MUST be declared; silent defaults are forbidden. The posture MUST agree with the corresponding row in artifacts/governance/residual_dependencies.yaml when that ledger declares a stricter posture (for example, a forbidden posture on a profile MUST NOT be relaxed to required by this matrix).

absence_impact.description: Short statement of what the user experiences when this dependency is unavailable. Pairs with the row's absence_narrows_to.

forced_input.description: At least one declared drift the lane runner replays under --force-drill.
